channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japanese space startup aborts 2nd satellite launch attempt minutes after liftoff
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese space startup said its second attempt to launch a rocket carrying satellites into orbit had been aborted minutes after liftoff Wednesday and destroyed itself again, nine months after the company's first launch attempt ended in an explosion. Space One's Kairos No.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: As wars rage around them, Armenian Christians in Jerusalem's Old City feel the walls closing in
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — As the war in Gaza rages, Syria's government transforms, and the Israeli-occupied West Bank seethes, Armenian residents of the Old City of Jerusalem fight a different battle — one that is quieter, they say, but no less existential.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Verdicts are due in the historic French rape trial that turned Gisèle Pelicot into a feminist hero
item.description: AVIGNON, France (AP) — French judges plan to deliver hugely anticipated verdicts this week in a historic drugging-and-rape trial that has turned the victim, Gisèle Pelicot, into a feminist hero.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's borders have shifted throughout its history. Action in Syria may reshape them again
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, entered Syrian territory Tuesday and said Israeli troops would remain in the area indefinitely, blurring the border with its northern neighbor. Since its establishment in 1948, Israel has never had fully recognized borders.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from an Indonesian firing squad returns home
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina woman who had been on death row in Indonesia, and who was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015, arrived home early Wednesday after longstanding requests from her home country.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI and TERESA CEROJANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ontario's premier says Trump's tariffs would be a disaster for US markets
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The premier of Canada's most populous province said Tuesday President-elect Donald Trump's plan to impose sweeping 25% tariffs on all Canadian products would be a "disaster" that would hurt U.S. stock markets.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Herschel Walker as ambassador to Bahamas
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he would nominate former NFL player and one-time Georgia Senate candidate Herschel Walker to serve as his ambassador to the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel plans an extended occupation of Syrian buffer zone
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rescuers dig for survivors of Vanuatu earthquake that killed 14 people and injured more than 200
item.description: Vanuatu’s capital is without water after reservoirs were destroyed by a violent magnitude 7.3 earthquake that wrought havoc on the South Pacific island nation, killing at least 14 people and injuring hundreds, with the figure expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Global demand spurring Indonesia's mining boom comes at a cost for many communities
item.description: KABAENA, Indonesia (AP) — The crystal blue waters that once surrounded Kabaena are murky brown now, and the octopi and colorful fish that locals used to catch nearby to eat and sell have fled. The lush seaweed they used to harvest is gone.
item.dc:creator: YUSUF WAHIL and VICTORIA MILKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Authorities impose overnight curfew in cyclone-ravaged Mayotte as France rushes in aid
item.description: SAINT-DENIS, Reunion (AP) — French authorities on Tuesday announced an overnight curfew in Mayotte as they sought to stabilize the island territory in the aftermath of Cyclone Chido, the most intense storm to hit the Indian Ocean archipelago in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 14 dead and hundreds injured in magnitude 7.3 quake in Vanuatu. Some people are trapped in rubble
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A magnitude 7.3 earthquake that struck off Vanuatu killed at least 14 people, injured hundreds more and caused widespread damage across the South Pacific island nation, officials said early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly violence in Nigeria linked to breakup of United Methodist Church over LGBTQ policies
item.description: A religious schism has turned deadly in Nigeria, with a church member fatally shot and two young children killed as homes were set ablaze, according to United Methodist News Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian Santa swaps sleigh for Jet Ski to deliver toys — and joy — to disabled children in Rio
item.description: Dozens of children with disability gathered on Rio de Janeiro’s Copacabana beach on Tuesday to receive presents — and joy — from the white-bearded man, a joint effort by the city’s firefighters and nonprofit associations.
item.dc:creator: Lucas Dumphreys, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian opposition figure says fears of his mother's poisoning in Berlin proved false
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A prominent Russian opposition politician freed as part of the largest East-West civilian prisoner swap since the Cold War said Tuesday that fears of his mother's poisoning in Berlin have proven false. Vladimir Kara-Murza, a dual Russian-U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran pauses the process to implement a new, stricter headscarf law for women, official says
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran has paused the process of implementing a new, stricter law on women’s mandatory headscarf, or hijab, an official said — a bill that many believe could have reignited the protests that engulfed the Islamic Republic after the 2022 death of Mahsa Amini.
item.dc:creator: AMIR VAHDAT and NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen, Graham threaten sanctions on Turkey to achieve ceasefire with Syrian Kurdish allies
item.description: Sens. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) and Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) are threatening sanctions against Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan unless Ankara forces Syrian opposition fighters into a ceasefire with U.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy seriously damaged in Vanuatu earthquake
item.description: A forceful 7.4 magnitude earthquake shook the capital of Vanuatu damaging the newly established U.S. Embassy on Tuesday. Authorities reported the earthquake and subsequent aftershocks at 12:53 p.m. local time, according to an embassy alert.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli hostages' families hold Central Park rally, call on Biden, Trump to bring loved ones home
item.description: Family members of Israeli hostages met in New York City on Sunday to call for President Biden and President-elect Trump to bring them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of an American journalist imprisoned in Syria sees hope following news of Travis Timmerman's release
item.description: The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist captured in Syria, said the news of Travis Timmerman's recent prison release has sparked new hope that her son will soon walk free.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Major international figures who died in 2024
item.description: Numerous key international figures died in 2024, ranging from Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny to Yahya Sinwar, the leader of Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey seeks to purge pro-US Kurdish force that helped defeat Islamic State in Syria
item.description: A Turkish strongman exploits chaos in Syria to oust pro-American Syrian Kurds from border area while solidifying expansion in war-torn nation
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
